Title: Clean Code
Description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that teaches you how to write code that is clean, readable, and maintainable.

Title: The Pragmatic Programmer
Description: One of the most significant books on software development, covering career advice, technical practices, and methodologies.

Title: Design Patterns
Description: The legendary book that introduced design patterns to software engineering, outlining 23 classic solutions to common design problems.

Title: Introduction to Algorithms
Description: The comprehensive textbook and professional reference on computer algorithms, covering sorting, data structures, and graph theory.

Title: Refactoring
Description: The definitive guide to refactoring code, illustrating the principles and best practices with step-by-step examples.

Title: Crime and Punishment
Description: A masterpiece of Russian literature exploring morality, guilt, and redemption.

Title: The Brothers Karamazov
Description: A complex philosophical novel that enters deeply into questions of God, free will, and morality.

Title: The Idiot
Description: A novel exploring the impacts of a truly good and innocent man in a corrupt society.

Title: Notes from Underground
Description: Considered by many to be one of the first existentialist novels, exploring alienation and human nature.

Title: The Stranger
Description: A classic existentialist novel exploring the absurdity of human life through Meursault.

Title: The Plague
Description: A novel about a plague ravaging the Algerian city of Oran, exploring human resilience.

Title: The Myth of Sisyphus
Description: A philosophical essay introducing Camus's concept of the absurd and the value of existence.

Title: The Fall
Description: A philosophical novel written in the form of a dramatic monologue exploring guilt, freedom, and judgment.
A dystopian social science fiction novel about totalitarianism, surveillance, and individual freedom.

Title: Animal Farm
Description: A satirical allegorical novella reflecting events leading up to the Russian Revolution.

Title: Homage to Catalonia
Description: Orwell's personal account of his experiences during the Spanish Civil War.

Title: Down and Out in Paris and London
Description: A memoir about poverty in the two cities, reflecting Orwell's early struggles and social critiques.

Title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra
Description: A philosophical novel containing ideas of the Ubermensch and the death of God.

Title: Beyond Good and Evil
Description: A work that accuses past philosophers of lacking critical sense and blindly accepting dogmas.

Title: The Antichrist
Description: A critique of Christianity and its moral values from a philosophical standpoint.

Title: The Will to Power
Description: A book compiled from Nietzsche's notebooks outlining his thoughts on the will to power and nihilism.

Title: الزيبق
Description: في منطقة من العالم يسودها الفساد وتحديدا في دولة منعزلة عن دول المنطقة تسمى تجينو، تحمل بباطنها خير كنوز الأرض وأعلاهم علماء سيطر الطمع وعبادة المال على نفوس الطبقة الحاكمة والموالين أدى إلى تعاونهم مع مملكة الشمال لمحو ذاكرة الشعب لتزداد هذه الطبقة ثراء دون مقاومة بعد أن نفذ الاحتلال الكيميائي الذي استمر لعقود… حتى ظهور الزييق
